title: Landlords That Accept Section 8 in Cleveland
metaTitle: Landlords That Accept Section 8 in Cleveland
metaDescription: Looking for landlords that accept Section 8 in Cleveland? Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers. Book a showing today.
answerBox: Rent Finder Cleveland is a local rental team whose entire selection — 90+ rental homes across Cleveland — accepts Section 8 Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. Acceptance is not required citywide: Ohio has no statewide source-of-income law, and Cleveland itself has no local ordinance protecting voucher holders.

sections.h2: Which landlords in Cleveland accept Section 8?
body: There is no single public list of every Cleveland landlord that accepts vouchers — acceptance is decided property-by-property. **Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready**, across 90+ rental homes concentrated in East and Southeast Cleveland (Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, Buckeye-Shaker) with additional homes on the West side and in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio.
Rather than calling around to ask each landlord one by one, you can [book a showing](/p/book-showing) with us and know upfront that the voucher question is already answered.

sections.h2: Is a landlord in Cleveland legally required to accept Section 8?
body: Generally, no. **Ohio law generally does not include source-of-income as a protected class**, so a private landlord in most of the state, including the City of Cleveland, can lawfully decline to rent to a voucher holder on that basis alone (not legal advice; verify current law). Cleveland passed a broader 'Pay to Stay' ordinance in August 2024, but source-of-income protections were negotiated out of that ordinance and were not enacted, as of this writing (2026-07-04) — the city administration has signaled it may introduce separate legislation later.
That is a real gap for renters, and it's exactly the gap our voucher-friendly selection is built to close: you don't need to find out the hard way whether a specific listing will say yes.

sections.h2: Where does Section 8 have legal protection near Cleveland?
body: A handful of Cuyahoga County suburbs have passed their own source-of-income ordinances that make it illegal for a landlord to refuse a tenant solely because they use a voucher: **Cleveland Heights** (2021), **South Euclid**, **University Heights**, **Warrensville Heights**, and **Linndale**. The City of Cleveland is not on that list. Always confirm the current ordinance with the specific municipality before assuming protection applies, since this kind of local law can change.

rows: Pay to Stay ordinance (Aug. 2024) excluded source-of-income language
Decision is left to each landlord/municipality

sections.h2: How do I find a Section 8-friendly house or apartment in Cleveland?
body: The fastest path is a property manager who already states its voucher policy plainly rather than a listing site where acceptance is unclear per unit. Since our whole selection qualifies, you can browse our [Section 8 houses for rent in Cleveland](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/section-8-houses-for-rent-cleveland-ohio/) or the broader [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) list and know every result works with a voucher.
If you don't have a voucher yet, see our guide on [how to apply for Section 8 through CMHA](/cleveland-rentals/section-8/how-to-apply-section-8-cleveland-cmha/) to start the process.

sections.h2: What should I ask a landlord before touring with a voucher?
body: Confirm the unit is currently HUD-inspection-ready or willing to schedule an inspection, ask whether the rent fits within your voucher's payment standard for the bedroom size, and get any voucher-related agreement in writing before you sign a lease. With our homes, HUD-readiness is already built in across the selection, which removes one of the biggest delays voucher holders run into elsewhere.

sections.h2: Why does a landlord's Section 8 policy matter so much?
body: A landlord's willingness to accept a voucher affects almost every step of your search: how long it takes to find a unit, whether the home is ready for a HUD inspection on day one, and whether your voucher's payment standard and the unit's rent will line up cleanly. When that answer is uncertain, renters often lose weeks touring homes that ultimately won't work with their voucher.

That's the practical reason we built our available homes the way we did — with **every single home**, regardless of size or neighborhood, accepting vouchers and already meeting HUD-inspection standards. It turns 'will this landlord take my voucher' from a question you have to ask into one you never have to think about.

faq.q: Do all Rent Finder Cleveland homes accept Section 8?
faq.a: Yes, all of them. Our 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland — houses, duplexes, triplexes, and fourplexes — every one accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready. You don't need to ask about a specific listing before touring or applying.

faq.q: Can a Cleveland landlord legally refuse a Section 8 voucher?
faq.a: In most of Ohio, including the City of Cleveland, yes — Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law and Cleveland has no local ordinance covering vouchers, so a private landlord can generally decline to accept one (not legal advice; verify current law).

faq.q: Which Cleveland-area suburbs protect Section 8 voucher holders?
faq.a: Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale have local ordinances that ban refusing tenants based on voucher use. The City of Cleveland itself is not among them as of 2026-07-04, so always confirm the current ordinance with the municipality.

faq.q: Where are your Section 8-friendly homes located?
faq.a: Mostly on Cleveland's East and Southeast side, including Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker, plus a smaller number of homes on the West side and additional rental units in Akron, Lorain, and Elyria, Ohio. Every one of them, regardless of neighborhood, accepts Housing Choice Vouchers.

faq.q: Does accepting Section 8 mean I'm automatically approved?
faq.a: No. Accepting a voucher means the home will consider a voucher as a valid payment source and is inspection-ready; you'll still go through standard screening, and your voucher must fit the unit's rent within CMHA's payment standard for your household size.
